etymology.description: phonetic 叀 (original form) + semantic 心 (heart) -> 惠
etymology.interpretation: 惠 evolved from 叀 (original form) with the addition of 心 (heart) to signify kindness or benevolence. The heart component introduces the idea of sincere feelings, which naturally leads to the concept of kindness.

examples.chinese: 他是一个非常善良和热情的人(他是一個非常善良和熱情的人)。
examples.pinyin: Tā shì yī gè fēicháng shànliáng hé rèqíng de rén.
examples.english: He is a very kind and warm-hearted person.

examples.chinese: 她的微笑总是给人一种温暖和宽慰的感觉(她的微笑總是給人一種溫暖和寬慰的感覺)。
examples.pinyin: Tā de wēixiào zǒng shì gěi rén yī zhǒng wēnnuǎn hé kuānwèi de gǎnjué.
examples.english: Her smile always gives people a feeling of warmth and comfort.

examples.chinese: 他对待朋友总是非常慷慨和惠(他對待朋友總是非常慷慨和惠)。
examples.pinyin: Tā duìdài péngyǒu zǒng shì fēicháng kāngkǎi hé huì.
examples.english: He is always very generous and kind towards his friends.
